title: Structured Escalation & Abort Cleanup
requirements:
Categorize escalations for faster triage. Clean abort without orphaned state.

1. Create schemas/escalation.schema.json with fields:
   - ticket (PROJ-123 format), escalations array with: id (esc-NNN), timestamp (ISO 8601), category (enum: blocked_dependency, test_failure, merge_conflict, review_stall, secret_detected, infra_failure, contradictory_feedback, team_conflict, unknown), severity (enum: critical, high, medium), source_agent, stage, context object (summary, details, artifacts array, suggested_action), resolved (boolean), resolved_at (nullable ISO 8601)

2. Create scripts/escalate.sh:
   - Args: <ticket_id> <category> <severity> <source_agent> <stage> <summary> [--details '...'] [--suggested-action '...']
   - Writes runs/{ticket_id}/ESCALATION.json (appends to array if file exists)
   - Logs EVENT to run.log
   - Calls notify.sh with escalation event
   - Updates PRD.json status to 'escalated' via update-prd-status.sh
   - Output: structured JSON confirmation

3. Replace ad-hoc escalations in CLAUDE.md:
   - Replace all 'ESCALATE' instructions with escalate.sh calls specifying category + severity
   - Map existing triggers to categories per the improvement plan mapping table

4. Create scripts/abort-run.sh:
   - Args: <ticket_id> <reason>
   - Steps: check for open draft PR and close it, update PRD.json overall_status to 'aborted', write ESCALATION.json, log EVENT, call run-summary.sh with status 'aborted', if jira input: transition ticket back
   - Does NOT remove worktree or delete branches
   - Output: JSON summary of cleanup actions taken

5. Integrate abort into pause/stop:
   - Update CLAUDE.md: --stop now calls abort-run.sh for full cleanup
   - --pause checkpoints state but does NOT abort (existing behavior)
   - Update parse-args.sh to pass abort reason if provided

6. Update dashboard:
   - Update dashboard/server.js: include ESCALATION.json in artifact merge
   - Update dashboard/index.html: add Escalations tab per ticket with category badge, severity color, summary, suggested action, timestamp. Sort by severity (critical first)

7. Write tests:
   - Test escalate.sh with each category (expect valid ESCALATION.json)
   - Test escalate.sh appending to existing file (expect array grows)
   - Test abort-run.sh with open draft PR (expect PR closed)
   - Test abort-run.sh with no PR (expect graceful skip)
   - Test abort-run.sh preserves worktree
acceptance_criteria: schemas/escalation.schema.json exists with all required fields and enums
scripts/escalate.sh creates valid ESCALATION.json with category, severity, context
escalate.sh appends to existing ESCALATION.json without overwriting
escalate.sh logs EVENT and calls notify.sh
CLAUDE.md ESCALATE instructions replaced with escalate.sh calls with correct categories
scripts/abort-run.sh exists and handles PR close, PRD status update, logging
Dashboard shows Escalations tab with category badges and severity colors
